item.description: Director Professor Dokyung Song (송도경) (Email: dokyungs@yonsei.ac.kr) Students Yoonjeong Park (Undergraduate Intern) Yunsoo Jo (Undergraduate Intern) Ji Soo Jang (Undergraduate Intern) Hongsun Jang (Undergraduate Intern) Looking for students! See the announcement here.

Refereed Conference Papers Agamotto: Accelerating Kernel Driver Fuzzing with Lightweight Virtual Machine Checkpoints [paper] Dokyung Song, Felicitas Hetzelt, Jonghwan Kim, Brent Byunghoon Kang, Jean-Pierre Seifert, Michael Franz In USENIX Security Symposium (USENIX Security), August 2020. Distributed Heterogeneous N-Variant Execution [paper] Alexios Voulimeneas, Dokyung Song, Fabian Parzefall, Yeoul Na, Per Larsen, Michael Franz, Stijn Volckaert In International Conference on Detection of Intrusions and Malware, and Vulnerability Assessment (DIMVA), June 2020.

Automated Vulnerability Discovery The very first step towards having secure software systems is to find and fix (or sanitize) the vulnerabilities in them. This research project aims to develop new techniques to automatically scan software systems to find latent vulnerabilities. This project focuses on developing techniques to discover vulnerabilities more effectively, more efficiently, or both. Exploit Mitigation It is practically impossible to have perfectly secure software systems, even with all the pre-release testing (or sanitizing) efforts.
